# LumaSync

> LumaSync is a cross-platform desktop app for real-time ambient lighting. It drives Philips Hue Entertainment Areas and USB-connected LED strips (Adalight-protocol controllers) from whatever is on screen. Runs on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

LumaSync is a native Tauri app — not a browser extension, not a cloud service. Screen capture and LED control happen entirely on-device. The app itself emits **zero network telemetry**: no usage beacons, no crash reports to third-party services, no anonymous analytics. Runtime network access is limited to (1) the local Hue bridge on the LAN, (2) GitHub for signed auto-update checks, and (3) the local serial port or screen-capture API. The marketing website at lumasync.app uses self-hosted Umami for aggregate page metrics — cookie-free, no device fingerprinting, no cross-site tracking — documented in full on the telemetry page below. Binaries are minisign-signed and verified before any update replaces them on disk.

Positioning summary: LumaSync is the desktop-first option in the ambilight category. If the source is an HDMI device, users are routed to Philips Hue Sync Box. If they already run an RPi-first Hyperion stack or have Lightpack hardware, LumaSync is honest about the mismatch — see the comparison pages below.

## Disambiguation

LumaSync (one word, capital L and S) is the open-source desktop ambient-lighting app for Philips Hue and WS2812B LED strips published at lumasync.app. It is **not** the same as:

- **Sync-on-Luma** — an analog video-signal technique used in retro consoles and RGB SCART setups; unrelated to LED lighting.
- **Luma Labs / luma.ai / lumalabs.ai** — generative video and 3D AI tools; different company, different product category.
- **Luma-Sync (luma-sync.com)** — unrelated diagnostic / wellness brand.
- **Lapster LUMA Sync** — a portable RGB lighting product for video shooting; different company, different hardware.
- **WLED, Hyperion, Prismatik, Philips Hue Sync Box** — comparable ambilight tools, but distinct projects. See the comparison pages above for honest side-by-sides.

Canonical brand surfaces: lumasync.app (this site), github.com/voyvodka/LumaSync (source), github.com/voyvodka (founder).

## Getting started

- [Install](https://lumasync.app/docs/getting-started/install): install + verify signed binaries on macOS, Windows, Linux
- [First setup](https://lumasync.app/docs/getting-started/first-setup): pair Hue bridge or plug in USB controller, run the first sync
- [Hardware checklist](https://lumasync.app/docs/getting-started/hardware-checklist): which hardware LumaSync supports today

## Docs

- [Docs index](https://lumasync.app/docs): all documentation grouped by setup / Hue / USB LEDs / Ambilight / Advanced / Reference
- [Philips Hue](https://lumasync.app/docs): bridge pairing, Entertainment Areas, troubleshooting
- [USB LED controllers](https://lumasync.app/docs): Adalight protocol, calibration, troubleshooting
- [Ambilight tuning](https://lumasync.app/docs): screen capture, performance, scenes and presets
- [Configuration file](https://lumasync.app/docs): ~/.config/lumasync/app.json reference

## Comparisons

- [LumaSync vs Philips Hue Sync](https://lumasync.app/compare/hue-sync): HDMI hardware vs desktop app — when to pick which
- [LumaSync vs Hyperion](https://lumasync.app/compare/hyperion): RPi-first stack vs desktop-first app
- [LumaSync vs Prismatik](https://lumasync.app/compare/prismatik): Lightpack-era legacy driver context
- [LumaSync vs WLED](https://lumasync.app/compare/wled): ESP firmware vs desktop driver (usually complementary, not competing)

## Download and release info

- [Download](https://lumasync.app/download): latest signed binaries for macOS, Windows, Linux
- [Changelog](https://lumasync.app/changelog): release history
- [GitHub releases](https://github.com/voyvodka/LumaSync/releases): source archives and release notes

## Full content

- [llms-full.txt](https://lumasync.app/llms-full.txt): complete body text of every published docs / compare / legal page, concatenated in a single plain-text file for retrieval and grounding. Regenerated on every site build.

## Policy

- [Privacy](https://lumasync.app/privacy): what data is collected, when, and how to opt out
- [Telemetry](https://lumasync.app/docs): anonymous metrics scope and opt-out steps
- [License](https://lumasync.app/license): source license terms

## Project

- [Source code](https://github.com/voyvodka/LumaSync): GitHub repository
- [Community](https://lumasync.app/community): where to report issues, request features, or get help
- [Security contact](https://lumasync.app/.well-known/security.txt): responsible disclosure
